name: Elementary Data
pricing_notes: OSS CLI free; Elementary Cloud $300/mo; Enterprise custom

auth.notes: Elementary Cloud uses API token; OSS runs with dbt profile credentials

performance.notes: dbt-native; test runs during dbt job execution; anomaly detection runs post-dbt

agent_readiness.notes: dbt-native data observability. Adds anomaly detection, data freshness monitoring, and schema change alerts to dbt projects. Generates data quality reports from dbt test results. Slack/PagerDuty alerting. REST API for programmatic access to test results. Python CLI. Best if already using dbt — zero additional infrastructure needed for OSS tier.

gotchas: dbt-only — not useful without existing dbt project
Anomaly detection requires historical data accumulation (days/weeks before effective)
Cloud version required for dashboard UI and team collaboration features
Python CLI adds to dbt job runtime
best_when: dbt teams wanting data observability without switching to separate tools
Data quality monitoring as natural extension of existing dbt tests
Teams needing Slack alerts for freshness/anomaly/schema changes from dbt
Data engineering teams early in observability journey (low setup friction)
avoid_when: Non-dbt data stacks (use Monte Carlo, Soda, or Great Expectations instead)
Real-time streaming data quality (dbt batch model doesn't fit)
Complex ML-based anomaly detection requirements (simpler than Montecarlo)
summary: Elementary is a dbt-native data observability tool that adds anomaly detection, freshness monitoring, and schema change alerts to dbt projects with minimal setup. Generates data quality reports from dbt test results. OSS CLI is free; cloud adds dashboard/collaboration. Best for dbt teams wanting observability without adding separate infrastructure. Not suitable for non-dbt stacks.
